raw_consumer_statement:
I was notified by Citi Mastercard that there was suspected fraudulent charges. I called per request and verified that I did not make a $ XXXX charge to XXXX. 
Several weeks later I received a fine print, long written document from Citi that, in part, stated that if " disputed their finding of fraud '' I needed to sign the document, scan it and e-mail it back with a single code in the subject line. 
I was quited confused because of the wording, and the fact that I had thought I had resolved this over the phone ( Citi first flagged transaction as fraudulent for me ).

I called Citi to clarify the text. I read the text to the person ( call center ) representing Citi over the phone. They stated that " If I disputed the charge, I must sign the document, scan it and return via e-mail. '' I stated this did not seem correct because of the verbiage in the document. They repeated that if I disputed the charge, I must sign and return. 
I did so ( see attachment ). 
Apparently the code in the subject line triggered a computer to change status of charge from fraudulent to valid and despite many hours on the phone, they have not been able to fix this.
